title: Feature request: Repeater configuration to disable status LEDs

messages.author: ByteWelder
messages.text: It would be nice to have an option to turn off the activity LED lights on the repeater. My repeater is in my guest room and I currently have to wrap the case in aluminium foil so it doesn't light up the room at night. The light is problematic when I have a guest sleeping there. For reference: I'm using a Heltec V3 with the TonyG case: https://www.printables.com/model/561389-heltec-v3-case-for-meshtastic/files When the light LED goes on (flood being sent?) it lights up the entire case which is printed in white filament to fit better in the room. (edited)
messages.content_html: It would be nice to have an option to turn off the activity LED lights on the repeater. My repeater is in my guest room and I currently have to wrap the case in aluminium foil so it doesn't light up the room at night. The light is problematic when I have a guest sleeping there. For reference: I'm using a Heltec V3 with the TonyG case: https://www.printables.com/model/561389-heltec-v3-case-for-meshtastic/files When the light LED goes on (flood being sent?) it lights up the entire case which is printed in white filament to fit better in the room. (edited)
embeds_html: Heltec v3 case for Meshtastic by TonyG | Download free STL model | ...

messages.author: ByteWelder
messages.text: Similarly, it would be nice to be able to disable the OLED from waking up on certain events. e.g. a mode where only the button press would activate it.
messages.content_html: Similarly, it would be nice to be able to disable the OLED from waking up on certain events. e.g. a mode where only the button press would activate it.

messages.author: ByteWelder
messages.text: The 2 tiny LEDs near the display might not be, but I recall seeing a (rare) bright white flash. I suspect it's related to a flood message (incoming or outgoing) It's purely speculative though.
messages.content_html: The 2 tiny LEDs near the display might not be, but I recall seeing a (rare) bright white flash. I suspect it's related to a flood message (incoming or outgoing) It's purely speculative though.
